name: ION-14382 Visibility Audit OffsetDateTime Fix
description: Fix AuditAttributeConverter to decouple DynamoDB storage format (Z suffix) from REST API format (+0000) to resolve visibility module crash when reading audit.createdDateUtc from DynamoDB

entries.summary: Decoupled DynamoDB storage format from REST API format in AuditAttributeConverter. DynamoDB now writes Z suffix (matching production), REST API continues writing +0000 (for ETL). All 983 booking tests pass.
entries.detail:
Root cause: AuditAttributeConverter used a single OBJECT_MAPPER for both DynamoDB writes and reads. This OBJECT_MAPPER respected @JsonFormat(SSSZ) on Audit.java fields, producing "+0000" suffix for UTC timestamps in DynamoDB. The visibility module's SDK v1 OffsetDateTimeTypeConverter (from booking:2.1.8.M) cannot parse "+0000" — only "Z" or "+00:00".

Fix: Added DYNAMO_OBJECT_MAPPER with a custom OffsetDateTime serializer using DateTimeFormatter.ISO_DATE_TIME. This produces "Z" suffix for UTC in DynamoDB (matching production). The existing OBJECT_MAPPER is still used for reads (handles all formats). REST API continues using @JsonFormat(SSSZ) → "+0000" for ETL compatibility.

Files changed:
1. AuditAttributeConverter.java — Added DYNAMO_OBJECT_MAPPER with custom serializer, transformFrom() now uses it
2. AuditAttributeConverterTest.java — Updated EtlCompatibility → DynamoStorageFormat, corrected assertions from +0000 to Z
3. AuditAttributeConverterVisibilityCompatibilityTest.java — NEW: 23 tests covering visibility parsing simulation, DynamoDB format verification, REST API decoupling, round-trip with all historical formats

All 983 booking tests pass (0 failures).
